name: Chinese Food: Noodles
whatsNew: 8 noodle dishes from across China for the Chinese Food section — beef noodle soup, zhajiangmian, biangbiang noodles, cold sesame noodles, wonton noodles, crossing-the-bridge noodles, Guilin rice noodles and liangpi.

recipes.name: Beef Noodle Soup
recipes.description: A deeply savoury bowl of tender braised beef, wheat noodles and greens in a rich spiced broth — a national comfort dish with countless regional versions.
ingredients: 2 tbsp doubanjiang, 3 tbsp soy, 2 tbsp Shaoxing wine
Star anise, cinnamon, ginger, garlic, spring onion
Wheat noodles; bok choy to serve
steps: Blanch the beef, then brown with the aromatics and doubanjiang.
Add soy, wine, spices and water; simmer gently for 1.5-2 hours until tender.
Cook the noodles separately and blanch the greens.
Assemble the noodles in bowls, ladle over broth and beef, top with greens.

recipes.name: Zhajiangmian
recipes.description: Beijing's signature "fried sauce noodles" — thick wheat noodles under a savoury, salty fermented-bean and pork sauce, finished with fresh raw vegetables.
steps: Fry the pork until browned, add the bean pastes, wine and sugar.
Simmer the sauce gently until thick and glossy.
Boil the noodles and drain.
Top the noodles with the sauce and a pile of raw vegetables; mix before eating.

recipes.name: Biangbiang Noodles
recipes.description: Famously wide, belt-like Shaanxi hand-pulled noodles, finished with chili, garlic and scallion under a pour of smoking-hot oil that sizzles at the table.
ingredients: Hand-pulled wide noodles (flour, water, salt, rested dough)
2 tbsp chili flakes, 3 cloves garlic, 2 scallions
steps: Rest a firm dough, then stretch and slap into long wide ribbons; boil until just done.
Pile the noodles in a bowl and top with chili flakes, garlic and scallion.
Heat oil until smoking and pour it directly over the aromatics so they sizzle.
Add soy and vinegar and toss everything together.

recipes.name: Cold Sesame Noodles
recipes.description: Cooling noodles tossed in a nutty sesame-peanut sauce — the perfect no-cook lunch on a hot day.
ingredients: 2 tbsp sesame paste + 1 tbsp peanut butter
2 tbsp soy, 1 tbsp black vinegar, 1 tsp sugar
1 clove garlic, chili oil, shredded cucumber
steps: Cook the noodles, rinse under cold water and drain well.
Whisk the sesame paste, soy, vinegar, sugar and garlic, loosening with a little water.
Toss the noodles in the sauce.
Top with cucumber and a drizzle of chili oil.

recipes.name: Wonton Noodles
recipes.description: A Cantonese classic — springy thin egg noodles and plump shrimp wontons in a clear, fragrant broth.
ingredients: Chives or gai lan, white pepper
steps: Bring the broth to a gentle simmer and season lightly.
Poach the wontons until they float and turn translucent.
Briefly blanch the egg noodles so they stay springy.
Nest the noodles in bowls, add wontons and broth, finish with greens.

recipes.name: Crossing-the-Bridge Noodles
recipes.description: A Yunnan ritual: a bowl of scalding broth under a film of oil that keeps the heat in, into which you slip raw thin meats, vegetables and rice noodles to cook at the table.
ingredients: Rich chicken broth, kept very hot
Paper-thin raw chicken, pork or fish
steps: Heat the broth until almost boiling; the oil layer on top traps the heat.
Bring the broth to the table with the raw ingredients arranged separately.
Slide in the thin meats first — they cook in seconds in the hot broth.
Add the vegetables and rice noodles, then eat.

recipes.name: Guilin Rice Noodles
recipes.description: Smooth rice noodles dressed with savoury braised meat, crunchy pickles and peanuts — a beloved breakfast and snack from Guilin.
ingredients: Fried peanuts, scallions, a little braising 'lu' sauce
steps: Blanch the rice noodles until just tender and drain.
Toss with a spoonful of the savoury braising sauce.
Top with the sliced braised meat.
Pile on pickles, peanuts and scallions; mix before eating (broth optional on the side).

recipes.name: Liangpi (Cold Skin Noodles)
recipes.description: Wide, slippery, cold wheat-starch noodles tossed in a tangy, garlicky chili dressing with beansprouts and cucumber — a refreshing Shaanxi street snack.
ingredients: 3 tbsp black vinegar, 2 cloves garlic (in water), 2 tbsp chili oil
steps: Blanch the beansprouts and slice the cucumber.
Cut the cold-skin noodles into wide ribbons.
Dress generously with garlic water, vinegar, chili oil and a little salt.
Toss with the vegetables and serve cold.
